# 26 - SALES SCRIPTS - FUNNEL UNIVERSITY
## Russell Brunson | Scripts de Vendas Completos

> **Fonte:** `raw/17._Funnel_University_2016/3. Sales Scripts/`
> **Arquivos processados:** 6 transcrições (Perfect Webinar, Star Story Solution, OTO Bump, Who What Why How, Daily Seinfeld Emails, Perfect Webinar Funnel)
> **Data:** 2026-03-10
> **Elementos extraídos:** 32 (L1:5 L2:6 L3:7 L4:7 L5:7)

---

## L1 - PHILOSOPHIES (5)

**FIL-RB-SS-001** | The Stack Makes You Rich
> "Armand Morin walked me through what he did and said: 'When you close, do the stack. If you do the stack, you will be rich. If you don't, you're gonna bomb.' The stack is embarrassing to do — but it works every single time."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`
- Princípio: The Stack é o elemento que separa apresentações de vendas lucrativas das não lucrativas

**FIL-RB-SS-002** | Entertainment Over Education
> "As much as we think our audience wants to be educated, those who assume that are wrong. What your audience wants is they want to be entertained. That's it. And it's how you build a relationship with them."
- Fonte: `transcription_Daily_Seinfeld_Emails.txt`
- Princípio: Entreter vem antes de educar em email marketing. A educação é veículo, o entretenimento é o gancho.

**FIL-RB-SS-003** | Upsell = True Profit Center
> "Grocery stores take the first 28 days of the month to break even and the last three days is where they actually make their profit. Everything else you're doing in your business is there to break even — and upsells are where you start making your profit."
- Fonte: `transcription_OTO_Bump.txt`
- Princípio: Front-end = break even; upsell = real profit. Toda estratégia de front-end deve ser projetada para habilitar o upsell.

**FIL-RB-SS-004** | Webinar = Fastest Money Online
> "If someone said 'Russell, I need you to make $100,000 tomorrow or I'll kidnap your family' — I would put together a webinar. That's it. That is the fastest way to make money on the internet."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt`
- Princípio: Webinar sob demanda com lista existente é o instrumento financeiro mais rápido no marketing digital

**FIL-RB-SS-005** | One Thing Principle — All Content Serves One Belief
> "Everything, every single thing else I talk about — the only goal of those things is to re-emphasize over and over and over again this one thing. I'm not showing you this AND this AND this. I'm showing you THIS, and then all these secrets tie back and strengthen and solidify that one thing."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt`
- Princípio: Um webinar de sucesso tem UMA crença central que todos os secrets reforçam. Múltiplas mensagens diluem conversão.

---

## L2 - MENTAL MODELS (6)

**MM-RB-SS-001** | The Big Domino
> "There's one Big Domino — one thing that if they believe it, makes every other objection irrelevant. My whole webinar is about pushing over that one Big Domino. Every secret, every story, every proof point exists to knock over that one domino."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt` + `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`
- Modelo: Identificar a crença central que, se removida, elimina todas as objeções → construir o webinar inteiro ao redor de derrubar esse domino

**MM-RB-SS-002** | False Belief Patterns (3 Core)
> "There are three core false beliefs people have: (1) The Vehicle — they don't believe in the thing you're selling. (2) Internal — they don't believe in themselves. (3) External — they believe outside forces will stop them. Each secret is designed to destroy one false belief."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt`
- Modelo: Mapear 3 categorias de falsas crenças antes de construir o roteiro → cada secret ataca uma

**MM-RB-SS-003** | Buying Loop Psychology (OTO)
> "Before we learned the psychology: we'd say 'Thank you so much for your order! Here's a special offer.' As soon as you say 'thank you for your order' — they think the process is finished. The buying loop is closed. Now you're trying to reopen it — it's harder. Instead: 'Your order is NOT yet complete.'"
- Fonte: `transcription_OTO_Bump.txt`
- Modelo: Nunca fechar o loop de compra antes de apresentar o upsell. "Order not complete" mantém o estado mental de compra ativo.

**MM-RB-SS-004** | Attractive Character (Star Story Solution)
> "The star of the story should be where the viewer is NOW, going through what they're going through. You enter the story at the same point of pain they're at. The star's journey IS their potential journey. The transformation creates a bridge from their present to their desired future."
- Fonte: `transcription_Star__Story__Solution.txt`
- Modelo: O "star" da VSL não é você-hoje-bem-sucedido. É você-no-passado na mesma situação do prospect agora.

**MM-RB-SS-005** | Story Starts at High Drama
> "No good movie starts with 'so-and-so was born, then this happened...' — 15 boring minutes before anything interesting. They start with the crazy thing happening first. 'WHOA, what just happened?' Then they go back and fill in the backstory. All good stories start at a point of high drama."
- Fonte: `transcription_Star__Story__Solution.txt` (credit: Daegan Smith)
- Modelo: Começar qualquer story no ponto de máximo drama — depois preencher o contexto retroativamente

**MM-RB-SS-006** | Seinfeld Email Framework (Nothing = Everything)
> "The structure is: you pick a story that happened to you that day — it can be ANYTHING. That's why we call them Seinfeld emails. Like the show about nothing. At the end of the email, you tie the story back to your product/offer. The story can be totally unrelated — it's just the vehicle for entertainment."
- Fonte: `transcription_Daily_Seinfeld_Emails.txt`
- Modelo: Email diário de entretenimento: story qualquer → tie-in para oferta. Relação entre story e produto é irrelevante; a conexão vem do escritor, não da story.

---

## L3 - HEURISTICS (7)

**HEUR-RB-SS-001** | Perfect Webinar Ideal Length
> "The ideal webinar is 90 minutes. 60 minutes of content (3 secrets) and 30 minutes of close."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt`
- Benchmark: 90 min total = 60 min conteúdo + 30 min fecha

**HEUR-RB-SS-002** | Trial Close Frequency
> "Counter all my webinars — I've probably asked you to say yes at least 1,000 times in my presentation or more. Every time I share a testimonial, I drop 50 trial closes after it."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`
- Benchmark: Mínimo 1.000 micro-confirmações por webinar de 90 min ≈ 11+ por minuto no pico

**HEUR-RB-SS-003** | Webinar Show-Up Optimization
> "I change the webinar title in GoToWebinar to '** STARTING NOW **' 10-15 minutes before start and notify all participants — that single email with subject line 'Starting Now' is one of the biggest show-up tricks."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`
- Heurística: Mudança de título com notificação GTW 10-15min antes = spike de show-up

**HEUR-RB-SS-004** | OTO Conversion Real Numbers (DotCom Secrets Launch)
> "First upsell at $197 — got just under 5% conversion, 1,177 orders. Second upsell at $297 — 1.92% conversion, 416 orders at $300 each. That adds up when you're putting all traffic through."
- Fonte: `transcription_OTO_Bump.txt`
- Benchmark: Upsell 1 ($197): ~5% | Upsell 2 ($297): ~2% em lançamento de livro

**HEUR-RB-SS-005** | Who What Why How = 70% of Sales
> "This is the tool that we use to sell probably 70% of what we sell nowadays. Whenever I'm selling something free plus shipping, $7, or low ticket — this is the script."
- Fonte: `transcription_Who__What__Why__How.txt` (credit: John Carlton)
- Heurística: WWWH script cobre 70% dos casos de uso de vendas; Star Story Solution para produtos de ticket mais alto com story forte

**HEUR-RB-SS-006** | Stack Embarrassment = Sign It's Working
> "I started the stack and it got kind of ridiculous — people were laughing. But guess what? Everybody knew exactly what the offer was and they bought like crazy. Don't NOT do this because it feels embarrassing — it works so good."
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt`
- Heurística: Se o stack parece ridículo e exagerado, está no caminho certo. O constrangimento do apresentador = clareza para o comprador.

**HEUR-RB-SS-007** | Daily Email Revenue vs. Corporate Email
> "$100,000 consulting fee to teach Seinfeld emails. Client's corporate weekly email: tons of time + effort, makes no money. Seinfeld daily emails: 5-10 minutes each, highest revenue per email in the company."
- Fonte: `transcription_Daily_Seinfeld_Emails.txt`
- Heurística: Email diário de entretenimento gera mais receita por email que newsletter corporativa semanal

---

## L4 - FRAMEWORKS (7)

**FW-RB-SS-001** | Perfect Webinar Structure
```
PHASE 1: INTRO (10-15 min)
  - High energy loop before start (2-3 min pré-webinar)
  - Introduction + hook + big promise
  - The ONE THING: "How to [want] without [don't want]"
  - Tease the three secrets (don't reveal — just names)

PHASE 2: CONTENT — 3 SECRETS (45-50 min)
  - Each secret = destroy one false belief (Vehicle, Internal, External)
  - Each secret structure: Epiphany Bridge Story → New Belief
  - All secrets REINFORCE the same Big Domino
  - Trial closes throughout (micro "yes" confirmations)

PHASE 3: TRANSITION TO CLOSE (5 min)
  - Recap the one thing they now believe
  - Bridge to "here's how you can implement this"

PHASE 4: THE CLOSE — THE STACK (30 min)
  - Who this is for (call out your ICP)
  - Here's what you're getting: [item 1 + value]
  - Stack: item 1 + item 2 = value recap
  - Stack: + item 3 = new value total
  - [Continue stacking 5-7 elements]
  - Reveal price (embarrassingly low vs stacked value)
  - Closes: Disposable Income, Money Replenishes, Us vs Them, Reluctant Hero
  - Guarantee
  - Recap stack again → CTA
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt` + `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`

**FW-RB-SS-002** | The Stack (Armand Morin Method)
```
ELEMENT 1: Core product + value ($X)
  → "So you're getting [X], which is a $Y value"
ELEMENT 2: + Bonus #1 + value
  → "But wait, you ALSO get [bonus 1] — that alone is worth $Z"
  → RECAP: "So far that's [X + bonus 1] = $[X+Z] in value"
ELEMENT 3: + Bonus #2
  → RECAP total value
[Continue 5-7 elements]
PRICE REVEAL: "All of that for just $[fraction of total value]"
CLOSE + GUARANTEE → RECAP ENTIRE STACK AGAIN → BUY NOW
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt` + `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`

**FW-RB-SS-003** | Star Story Solution (VSL Script)
```
STAR: Introduce the "star" (attractive character at their LOW point = where viewer is NOW)
  - Pattern interrupt first (weird image, shocking statement)
  - "It wasn't always this way for me..."

STORY: The transformation journey
  - Start at HIGH DRAMA (most intense moment)
  - Go back and fill in backstory
  - Show the process/path from low to high
  - "It didn't just work for me..." → proof for others (case studies)
  - Hidden benefits (unexpected gains from transformation)

SOLUTION: Introduce the product as the mechanism
  - "Here's exactly what I did / what you can do"
  - Simple steps make it feel achievable
  - CTA
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Star__Story__Solution.txt` (credit: Daegan Smith for story structure)

**FW-RB-SS-004** | OTO/Upsell Script Framework
```
RULE 1: Never close the buying loop before upsell
  → Use "Your order is NOT yet complete"

RULE 2: Second yes is INFINITELY easier than first
  → Don't resell from scratch — piggyback on first purchase decision

RULE 3: Upsell logic: "You just got [X]. Want to make it even better?"
  → Congruent with what they just bought
  → Amplifies result of front-end purchase

UPSELL PAGE STRUCTURE:
  → Headline: "Wait! Your order is not complete"
  → "You just got [front-end] — here's the natural next step"
  → VSL or text + why this enhances their purchase
  → One-click add (same credit card, no re-entry)
  → CTA → Yes or No (never "no thanks" framing that dismisses)
```
- Fonte: `transcription_OTO_Bump.txt`

**FW-RB-SS-005** | Who What Why How (WWWH Script)
```
WHO: Who are you? (introduce yourself + credibility in 1-2 sentences)
WHAT: What do you have? (describe the product/offer clearly)
WHY: Why do they need it? (agitate pain + future pace benefit)
HOW: How can they get it? (CTA + specific action)

AMPLIFIERS (add after WHY/HOW):
  → Catch: What makes this different/unique
  → Urgency: Why now (deadline, limited spots, etc.)
  → Guarantee: Risk reversal
  → Recap: Restate WHO WHAT WHY HOW in 1 sentence
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Who__What__Why__How.txt` (credit: John Carlton)

**FW-RB-SS-006** | Daily Seinfeld Email Framework
```
STEP 1: Story about anything that happened to you today
  - Can be totally random (kid's soccer game, bad coffee, podcast you heard)
  - No need to relate to business topic upfront
  - Keep it entertaining — write like you talk

STEP 2: The Tie-In
  - "And that reminded me of [business principle / product]..."
  - Connect the mundane story to your offer/topic
  - The weirder the connection, the more memorable

STEP 3: CTA (every single email)
  - Every daily email has ONE call to action
  - Never a corporate multi-link recap email
  - One story → one link → one offer

FREQUENCY: Daily (yes, every single day)
LENGTH: 200-400 words max (write fast, send fast)
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Daily_Seinfeld_Emails.txt`

**FW-RB-SS-007** | False Belief → New Belief Transformation (Per Secret)
```
FOR EACH SECRET:
  STEP 1: Identify the false belief (Vehicle / Internal / External)
  STEP 2: "I used to believe [false belief]..."
  STEP 3: Epiphany Bridge Story: moment you discovered the new belief
  STEP 4: New belief statement
  STEP 5: Proof it's true (case study, data, demo)
  STEP 6: Trial close: "Does that make sense? Are you starting to see how this works?"

3-SECRET STRUCTURE:
  Secret 1 → Destroys Vehicle False Belief (about the method/product)
  Secret 2 → Destroys Internal False Belief (about themselves)
  Secret 3 → Destroys External False Belief (about circumstances)
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt`

---

## L5 - METHODOLOGIES (7)

**MET-RB-SS-001** | Building the Perfect Webinar (Step-by-Step)
```
FASE 1 — IDENTIFY THE ONE THING
  - Formula: "How to [desired result] without [biggest objection]"
  - Example: "How to add high-ticket sales without personally talking to anyone"
  - Everything in the webinar serves THIS statement

FASE 2 — MAP 3 FALSE BELIEFS
  - Vehicle: "This method/vehicle won't work because..."
  - Internal: "I personally can't do this because..."
  - External: "My situation/market/circumstances won't allow..."

FASE 3 — CRAFT 3 SECRETS (one per false belief)
  - For each: Epiphany Bridge Story → destroys false belief → new belief
  - Structure: Personal story → new insight → their situation → trial close

FASE 4 — BUILD THE STACK
  - List everything in the offer (product + all bonuses)
  - Assign realistic market values to each element
  - Practice saying them with value recap each time
  - Total value should be 10-20x the actual price

FASE 5 — CHOOSE CLOSES
  - Select 4-6 closes from: Disposable Income, Money Replenishes,
    Us vs Them, Reluctant Hero, Herd, If/Then, Future Pacing
  - Sequence: social proof first → logic closes → urgency → CTA

FASE 6 — INDOCTRINATION SEQUENCE
  - 3 pre-webinar videos (Mon/Tue/Wed before live Wed)
  - Each video builds belief in One Thing
  - Increases show-up rate and engagement
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar.txt` + `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`

**MET-RB-SS-002** | Webinar Launch Sequence (Registration to Live)
```
DAY 0 (Monday): Registration confirmed email
DAY 1 (Monday): Indoctrination Video #1 (builds belief in vehicle)
DAY 2 (Tuesday): Indoctrination Video #2 (destroys biggest objection)
DAY 3 (Wednesday morning): Indoctrination Video #3 (urgency/excitement)
DAY 3 (Wednesday 1hr before): Reminder email + SMS text
DAY 3 (Wednesday 10-15min before):
  → Change GTW title to "** STARTING NOW **"
  → Check "notify all participants" → GTW sends "Starting Now" email
  → Log in early and run high-energy loop
  → Start 2-3 minutes late to maximize attendance
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Perfect_Webinar_Funnel.txt`

**MET-RB-SS-003** | Writing a VSL (Star Story Solution)
```
FASE 1 — IDENTIFY YOUR STAR
  - Who is the attractive character? (you, a client, a fictional persona)
  - What is their LOW POINT that matches where the viewer is NOW?

FASE 2 — OPEN AT HIGH DRAMA
  - Most intense/critical moment of the journey
  - "I was sitting in my car, $200k in debt, staring at my phone..."
  - Do NOT start from the beginning — start at the crisis

FASE 3 — FILL IN BACKSTORY
  - How did they get to that crisis?
  - What had they tried that didn't work?

FASE 4 — THE TRANSFORMATION PATH
  - Discovery of the mechanism/solution
  - Results: obvious benefits + hidden benefits (unexpected gains)

FASE 5 — PROOF IT WORKS FOR OTHERS
  - "It didn't just work for me — here's [person] who did it too..."
  - 2-3 case studies of different types of people

FASE 6 — THE SOLUTION (OFFER)
  - "Here's exactly how you can do this..."
  - WWWH close: Who What Why How
  - CTA
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Star__Story__Solution.txt`

**MET-RB-SS-004** | Building Daily Email System
```
FASE 1 — COMMIT TO DAILY CADENCE
  - Every single day, same time
  - Sunday counts (some of highest opens)
  - Never skip — consistency > perfection

FASE 2 — STORY CAPTURE HABIT
  - Every day: note 1 thing that happened (kid, news, movie, random thought)
  - Takes 30 seconds — just a note on phone

FASE 3 — WRITE THE EMAIL (5-10 minutes)
  - Open: The story/observation (2-3 paragraphs)
  - Middle: The tie-in ("this reminded me of...")
  - Close: One CTA + one link

FASE 4 — SUBJECT LINE
  - Always related to the story, NOT the offer
  - Curiosity-based, not promotional
  - Never "SALE" or "DISCOUNT" — always story-based

FASE 5 — MONITOR AND ADJUST
  - Watch which stories get most replies
  - More stories like those
  - Never remove people who complain — they're engaging
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Daily_Seinfeld_Emails.txt`

**MET-RB-SS-005** | Upsell Funnel Construction
```
FASE 1 — MAP THE UPSELL SEQUENCE (before launch)
  - Front-end → Upsell 1 → Downsell 1 (if decline) → Upsell 2 → Downsell 2
  - Upsell 1: natural next step (~$97-197)
  - Upsell 2: premium version or bundle (~$297-497)
  - Each downsell: payment plan or stripped version of declined upsell

FASE 2 — WRITE UPSELL PAGES
  - Headline: "Wait! Your Order Is NOT Complete"
  - Reference front-end purchase immediately
  - Video: 3-5 minutes max (they're already in buy mode)
  - One-click add (same billing info)

FASE 3 — TEST AND OPTIMIZE
  - Benchmark: U1 ~5%, U2 ~2% (DotCom Secrets launch data)
  - If U1 < 3% → test new headline, new video
  - Never pull the page — optimize first

FASE 4 — COMPLIANCE
  - "Your order IS complete" must be true for the front-end
  - "Not complete" only valid if genuinely optional add-on
```
- Fonte: `transcription_OTO_Bump.txt`

**MET-RB-SS-006** | Pattern Interrupt Creation for VSL/Ads
```
FASE 1 — IDENTIFY THE UNEXPECTED
  - Find something visually or conceptually jarring in your niche
  - Rule: "What would make someone stop scrolling and ask 'what is that?'"
  - Examples: two-headed buffalo, goldfish, weird statistic

FASE 2 — CONNECT TO OUTCOME
  - "What does [weird thing] have to do with [desired outcome]?"
  - The connection can be loose — the curiosity gap is the hook

FASE 3 — TEST FORMATS
  - Video: animated + surprising visual first 2 seconds
  - Image: weird/unexpected photo
  - Headline: statement that seems wrong or contradictory

FASE 4 — APPLY TO ALL ENTRY POINTS
  - Ads: pattern interrupt in first frame
  - Email subject: pattern interrupt in subject line
  - Webinar title: "How to [counterintuitive outcome]"
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Star__Story__Solution.txt`

**MET-RB-SS-007** | WWWH Sales Script for Low-Ticket/Lead Gen
```
WHO (10-20 seconds):
  "Hi, I'm Russell Brunson, author of DotCom Secrets and founder of ClickFunnels..."
  [Credibility in 1 sentence, only what's relevant to THIS offer]

WHAT (10-20 seconds):
  "I have a FREE book called DotCom Secrets that teaches you..."
  [Product name + one-sentence description of what it IS]

WHY (30-60 seconds):
  "If you're struggling to get traffic/sales/leads, this book shows you..."
  [Pain → solution → future state]
  "People like [name] went from X to Y using this..."
  [One quick proof point]

HOW (15-30 seconds):
  "Click the button below, tell me where to ship it, and I'll send it free"
  [Specific action + what happens next]

AMPLIFIERS:
  CATCH: "The only thing you pay is $7.95 shipping"
  URGENCY: "We're running low on copies / offer expires [date]"
  GUARANTEE: "If you don't love it, I'll refund your shipping"
  RECAP: "Free book, just pay shipping, ships tomorrow — click below"
```
- Fonte: `transcription_Who__What__Why__How.txt`

---

## RESUMO DE CLOSES DO PERFECT WEBINAR

| Close | Aplicação | Momento |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| **Disposable Income** | "Take this asset vs spend on liabilities" | Mid-stack |
| **Money Replenishes** | "Money comes back every 2 weeks — your time doesn't" | Mid-stack |
| **Us vs Them** | "Two types of people on this webinar..." | Before final CTA |
| **Reluctant Hero** | "I'm nothing special — if I can do this, you can" | Before CTA |
| **Future Pacing** | "Imagine in 90 days when..." | After stack reveal |
| **Herd Close** | "1,200 people have signed up in the last hour" | Live urgency |
| **If/Then** | "If you believe [X] is possible, then the next step is..." | Transition to close |
